column2: Mental healthcare must be included in universal healthcare coverage (psychiatric, psychological, therapy)
Covered treatments must be protected from partisan political interference
Coverage decisions must be grounded in evidence-based scientific research
Funding for clinical trials and approvals of new treatments
Streamline approval processes for new treatments while maintaining safety
Establish stricter quality standards for ingredients in generic medications
Ban non-essential common allergens in medications where not medically necessary
Fund research on safety and efficacy of common nutritional supplements
Create standardized labeling for supplements including full ingredient disclosure
Require lab testing transparency and quality certification for supplements
Establish minimum quality and testing standards for supplements
Require full traceability of drugs and supplements across supply chain (batch, manufacturing, shipping, handling)
Emergency medical services must be free for all individuals, regardless of citizenship status
Free emergency care includes ambulances, first responders, hospital care, medical supplies, and follow-up care
Full healthcare coverage applies to citizens and long-term/permanent residents
Residency requirement: minimum 2 years for elective or non-life-threatening procedures
Exceptions to residency requirement for life-threatening or disability-causing conditions
Establish an independent scientific governance body for determining covered treatments and standards of care
Body must be insulated from partisan political control
Appointments must be staggered to prevent single administration capture
Appointment structure should include both federal and state-level participation
Multi-layer guardrails required to prevent political and industry capture
Decision-making must be based on transparent, evidence-based scientific standards
Public transparency required for methodologies, decisions, and conflicts of interest
Strict conflict-of-interest rules for members (financial, industry ties, advisory roles)
Independent audit mechanisms required for oversight of decisions and processes
Separate scientific evaluation from political funding decisions wherever possible
Healthcare standards should be determined by an independent, multi-layered system separating research, approval, and coverage decisions
Governance should include both federal and state-appointed members with staggered terms
Decisions must be transparent, evidence-based, and include published reasoning and dissenting views
Strong conflict-of-interest restrictions required for all decision-makers
Healthcare policy and coverage decisions must not selectively omit or exclude relevant scientific data
Conclusions used to justify healthcare policy must be directly supported by the full body of available evidence
Require full transparency of all data sources, methodologies, and exclusions used in healthcare decision-making
Require publication of dissenting analyses or minority scientific opinions when significant disagreement exists
Establish independent review mechanisms to audit scientific integrity of major healthcare decisions
Explicit safeguards against ideological or political distortion of scientific findings
Telemedicine services must not be restricted by state or regional licensing boundaries when providing covered care
Telemedicine must be fully integrated into coverage and treated as equivalent to in-person care where medically appropriate
Establish explicit legal protections for journalists engaged in lawful newsgathering and reporting
Protect the confidentiality of journalistic sources (shield law protections) at the federal level
Government may not compel disclosure of sources except under narrowly defined, high-threshold conditions
Prohibit retaliation, surveillance, or prosecution of journalists for publishing information of public interest, except in clearly defined and limited cases
Prohibit use of national security or classification systems to conceal wrongdoing, corruption, or illegal activity
Require independent judicial review before any action to compel disclosure of journalistic materials or communications
Limit use of subpoenas, warrants, or surveillance tools targeting journalists or their communications
Extend protections to modern forms of journalism, including independent journalists and digital media creators
Require transparency and reporting when government actions target journalists or news organizations
Treat substance use disorder as a medical condition rather than a criminal issue
Implement harm reduction strategies (e.g., safe-use education, overdose prevention, access to treatment resources)
Establish national standards and regulations for rehabilitation and treatment facilities
Require evidence-based treatment practices in all licensed rehabilitation facilities
Prohibit abusive, coercive, or non-evidence-based practices in rehabilitation programs
Require transparency in outcomes, success rates, and treatment methodologies for rehab facilities
Ensure accessibility and affordability of treatment services under universal healthcare coverage
Provide oversight, auditing, and enforcement mechanisms for rehab facilities
Expand availability of voluntary treatment programs and reduce reliance on court-mandated programs
